text.tspan: Deploying agent changes (new prompt, new model, new tools) is RISKY.
Quality is hard to measure pre-deployment; users notice changes.
Rainbow deployment: run K variants IN PRODUCTION, each on a slice of traffic.
Collect per-variant metrics. Promote the winner; roll back the rest.
Deploy new prompt / model / tools to 100% of traffic.
If it's worse, you find out from user complaints.
Rollback takes hours; damage is done.
Variant A (control): 70% of traffic, current prompt + Sonnet
Variant B: 10% of traffic, new prompt + Sonnet
Variant C: 10% of traffic, current prompt + new Sonnet version
Variant D: 10% of traffic, new prompt + new model
Per-variant metrics:
Cost / query
Latency p50, p95
Eval score
User feedback
Error rate
Daily: review per-variant metrics.
A variant wins -> promote (shift traffic).
A variant fails -> roll back (drop traffic to 0).
A variant ties -> keep running until significance.
Like feature flags + experimentation,
adapted to LLM-specific axes (model, prompt, tools).
